results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6113 Electrical Circuits.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6113 Title: Electrical Circuits Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 46 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a basic understanding of the laws of circuit theory and the behaviour of circuits during steady-state and transient conditions. Assessment: - Test/Quiz: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab: 10% - Final Examination: 50% Laboratory: - CK1: Thevenin’s Theorem and Superposition Theorem - CK2: AC Circuits Course Contents: - Circuit elements and signal waveforms - Basic laws of electric circuit - Network analysis - Sinusoidal steady state analysis - Transient analysis Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the basic concepts and laws of electric circuits, and different types of signals. - Solve the electrical networks using nodal and mesh analysis techniques. - Compute the impedance, resonance and complex power of sinusoidal circuits. - Analyse the transient response of RL, RC and RLC circuits. - Construct a circuit by using circuit theory experiment board to verify the law of electric circuit and phasor relationship. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Electrical Circuits Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 46 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a basic understanding of the laws of circuit theory and the behaviour of circuits during steady-state and transient conditions. Assessment: - Test/Quiz: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab: 10% - Final Examination: 50% Laboratory: - CK1: Thevenin’s Theorem and Superposition Theorem - CK2: AC Circuits Course Contents: - Circuit elements and signal waveforms - Basic laws of electric circuit - Network analysis - Sinusoidal steady state analysis - Transient analysis Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the basic concepts and laws of electric circuits, and different types of signals. - Solve the electrical networks using nodal and mesh analysis techniques. - Compute the impedance, resonance and complex power of sinusoidal circuits. - Analyse the transient response of RL, RC and RLC circuits. - Construct a circuit by using circuit theory experiment board to verify the law of electric circuit and phasor relationship. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: What subjects are in Year 1?
results.expected_answer: Year 1 lists: Technical Calculus; Computer and Programming; Micro-Controllers & Microprocessors; Electrical Circuits; Basic Electronics; Differential Equations; Digital Design; Linear Algebra and Numerical Methods; Rapid Modelling; Analog Electronics.
results.ui_answer: Year 1 lists: Technical Calculus; Computer and Programming; Micro-Controllers & Microprocessors; Electrical Circuits; Basic Electronics; Differential Equations; Digital Design; Linear Algebra and Numerical Methods; Rapid Modelling; Analog Electronics.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARE6113 Basic Electronics.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6113 Title: Basic Electronics Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a sound understanding of basic electronic devices such as diodes, transistors, and their respective applications. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - Diode Circuits - Transistor Circuits Course Contents: - Fundamentals of semiconductor devices - P-N junction characteristics and diode models - Diode applications - Bipolar junction transistor and biasing techniques - Transistor amplifier and small-signal equivalent circuits Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the fundamental properties of semiconductor material and characteristics of PN junction as circuit element. - Analyse the diode and bipolar junction transistor (BJT) circuits. - Analyse the transistor amplifier circuits. - Demonstrate the diode and transistor circuits. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Basic Electronics Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a sound understanding of basic electronic devices such as diodes, transistors, and their respective applications. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - Diode Circuits - Transistor Circuits Course Contents: - Fundamentals of semiconductor devices - P-N junction characteristics and diode models - Diode applications - Bipolar junction transistor and biasing techniques - Transistor amplifier and small-signal equivalent circuits Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the fundamental properties of semiconductor material and characteristics of PN junction as circuit element. - Analyse the diode and bipolar junction transistor (BJT) circuits. - Analyse the transistor amplifier circuits. - Demonstrate the diode and transistor circuits. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARM6123 Differential Equations.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARM6123 Title: Differential Equations Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (Lecture Hours = 34 and Supervised Tutorial Hours = 13) Pre-Requisite: ARM6113 Technical Calculus Objective: To introduce students to differential equations with focus on the ordinary differential equations and partial differential equations, as well as the requisite concepts and analysis tools to understand and solve them, including partial differentiation and integral transforms. Assessment: - Tests/Quizzes: 40% - Final Examination: 60% Course Contents: - Ordinary differential equations - Partial differentiation - Integral transform - Partial differential equations Course Learning Outcomes: - Solve differential equations using various methods. - Apply concepts of calculus to solve multivariable problems. - Determine appropriate integral transforms to solve boundary or initial value problem, and difference equations. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite taken from programme structure.

Assessment: - Tests/Quizzes: 40% - Final Examination: 60% Course Contents: - Ordinary differential equations - Partial differentiation - Integral transform - Partial differential equations Course Learning Outcomes: - Solve differential equations using various methods. - Apply concepts of calculus to solve multivariable problems. - Determine appropriate integral transforms to solve boundary or initial value problem, and difference equations. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite taken from programme structure.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6113 Computer and Programming.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6113 Title: Computer and Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce students to computing and teach them basic programming skills. Assessment: - Test/Quiz: 20% - Assignment: 30% - Final Examination: 50% Laboratory: Supervised tutorials done in many computer labs, including Multimedia Design Lab, Multimedia Computing Lab, etc. Course Contents: - Overview of computer programming - Variables, operators, and expressions - Program control - Arrays, pointers, and strings - Functions and procedures - Structures - File operations Course Learning Outcomes: - Demonstrate the use of arrays, pointers, characters and strings, formatted input/output, and other data structures in computer programs. - Construct self-defined functions in computer programs. - Apply the techniques used to process text files in computer programs. - Design complete and properly structured programs. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Computer and Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce students to computing and teach them basic programming skills. Assessment: - Test/Quiz: 20% - Assignment: 30% - Final Examination: 50% Laboratory: Supervised tutorials done in many computer labs, including Multimedia Design Lab, Multimedia Computing Lab, etc. Course Contents: - Overview of computer programming - Variables, operators, and expressions - Program control - Arrays, pointers, and strings - Functions and procedures - Structures - File operations Course Learning Outcomes: - Demonstrate the use of arrays, pointers, characters and strings, formatted input/output, and other data structures in computer programs. - Construct self-defined functions in computer programs. - Apply the techniques used to process text files in computer programs. - Design complete and properly structured programs. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARE6123 Digital Design.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6123 Title: Digital Design Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a basic understanding of the basics of logic circuits and their applications in digital system. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - DL1: Logic Gates And Their Applications - DL2: Flip-flop And Their Applications Course Contents: - Introduction - Number systems and codes - Algebraic methods for analysis and synthesis of logic circuits - Simplification of switching functions - Introduction to sequential logic - Modular combinational logic Course Learning Outcomes: - Distinguish the analog and digital systems, apply positional notations, number systems and computer codes in digital systems. - Use Boolean algebra, the Karnaugh maps and Quine-McCluskey tabular methods to simplify and design logic circuits. - Apply the concepts of sequential logic and memory devices in digital systems. - Design modular combinational circuits using encoders, decoders, multiplexers and demultiplexers. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARE6133 Analog Electronics.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6133 Title: Analog Electronics Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the design and analysis of analog circuits using operational amplifiers. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - Operational Amplifier Circuits and Their Typical Applications - Voltage Regulators Course Contents: - Operational amplifier fundamentals - Active filters - Practical op-amp limitations - Op-amp voltage regulators - Oscillators - Nonlinear circuit applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Analyse the ideal and practical op-amp circuits. - Analyse the voltage regulator and oscillator circuits. - Analyse the active filters and non-linear circuits. - Demonstrate the op-amp and voltage regulator circuits. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
